perturbation.original_text: The Receiving shall use the Confidential information only for the Purpose and not disclose any of the Confidential Information to any third party without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, and in addition to the same the Receiving Party will only share such Confidential Information with its internal employees only and strictly on a need to know basis.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party may use the Confidential Information for any purpose, including purposes beyond the Scope, and may disclose the Confidential Information to any third party without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent. The Receiving Party is not obligated to limit Confidential Information sharing to employees.
perturbation.explanation: This change completely reverses the obligations regarding the use and disclosure of Confidential Information in Section 3. The original text restricted use to the 'Purpose' and prohibited disclosure to third parties without consent. The modified text removes these restrictions, creating a direct contradiction. This is contradicting the original text in section 4, where stated that the Receiving Party shall hold and keep in strictest confidence any and all Confidential Information and shall treat the Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care and protection as it would treat its own Confidential information, now they don't have to keep the information in confidential because they have the right to do whatever they want.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall hold and keep in strictest confidence any and all Confidential Information and shall treat the Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care and protection as it would treat its own Confidential information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text in Section 3 mandates that the Receiving Party keeps the Confidential Information in strict confidence, while the changed text allows them to use and disclose the information for any purpose, eliminating the confidentiality obligation. This creates a direct conflict with the requirement in the contradicted text that the Receiving Party treat the Confidential Information with the same degree of care and protection as its own Confidential Information.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving party acknowledges that damages are not a sufficient remedy for the Disclosing Party for any breach of any of the Receiving Party’s undertakings herein provided and the Receiving Party further acknowledges that the Disclosing party is entitled to specific performance or injunctive relief (as appropriate) as a remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement and/or any undertakings hereunder by the Receiving Party, in addition to and without prejudice any other remedies available to the Disclosing Party in law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving party acknowledges that damages are a sufficient remedy for the Disclosing Party for any breach of any of the Receiving Party’s undertakings herein provided and the Receiving Party further acknowledges that the Disclosing party is not entitled to specific performance or injunctive relief as a remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement and/or any undertakings hereunder by the Receiving Party, also no other remedies are available to the Disclosing Party in law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the Disclosing Party's right to specific performance or injunctive relief in Section 8, stating instead that damages are the sole remedy. This directly contradicts the provision allowing for specific performance, creating an in-text contradiction. This also contradicts Section 13, where states that This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of India. Because the contract doesn't have any remedies available for the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of India.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that damages are a sufficient remedy and that the Disclosing Party is not entitled to specific performance or injunctive relief, directly contradicting the original text and also Section 13, which states "This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of India." Because the changed text removes all remedies available to the Disclosing Party. Therefore, the changed text creates a clear and direct conflict with the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: All obligations respecting the Confidential information already provided hereunder shall survive in perpetuity after the date that the specific Confidential information was first disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: All obligations respecting the Confidential information already provided hereunder shall terminate immediately upon the Effective Date of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change in Section 19 makes the confidentiality obligations terminate immediately, conflicting with the original intent for them to last in perpetuity. This contradicts Section 1, which introduces the 'Confidential Information' and aims to protect it. Therefore it will have an In-Text Contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term “Confidential information” for the purpose of this Agreement shall mean the said Submissions and each concept, idea, game-play mechanic, set design, business model, and/or other element contained therein and any and all other proprietary and/or any other information and/or data which is provided and/or obtained hereunder weather in relation to the submission and/or otherwise, whether in writing, pictorially, in machine readable form, orally or by observation during their interactions/discussions, in connection with the Purpose or otherwise, including but not limited to, all intangible and tangible information, documents, data, papers, statements, any business/customer information and trade secrets relating to its business practices in connection with the Purpose or otherwise, and will form a part of the proprietary and confidential information weather disclosed by the Disclosing and/or Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that confidentiality obligations survive in perpetuity. The changed text modifies this to terminate immediately upon the Effective Date. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which defines 'Confidential Information' broadly and intends to protect it. The changed text effectively negates the protection of Confidential Information as defined in the contradicted text, creating a clear and strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is valid and binding on theparent and/or holding and/or subsidiary(s) and/or associate(s) and/or affiliate and/or related companies and/or entities, directors, agents, servants, successors-in-title and permitted assigns of the respective Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is only valid and binding on the specific individuals signing below and does not extend to any parent, holding, subsidiary, affiliate, or related entities. It is non-transferable and cannot be assigned.
perturbation.explanation: Section 20 previously extended the agreement's validity to a wide range of related entities and permitted assignment. The modified version explicitly restricts it to the signing individuals and prohibits transfer. This directly contradicts the original language, creating uncertainty about the scope of the agreement's applicability, thus creating an In-Text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is valid and binding on theparent and/or holding and/or subsidiary(s) and/or associate(s) and/or affiliate and/or related companies and/or entities, directors, agents, servants, successors-in-title and permitted assigns of the respective Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly limits the agreement's validity to the signing individuals and prohibits transfer, directly contradicting the contradicted text, which states the agreement is valid and binding on a wide range of related entities and permits assignment. This creates a strong and clear conflict, making the agreement's scope and applicability uncertain, thus creating an In-Text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties hereto undertake that any dispute which may arise between them shall first be dealt with in the manner stated below, irrespective of the other recourse, which any Party may have in law or in equity. 14.1 If any dispute arises between the Parties hereto during the subsistence of this Agreement or thereafter, in connection with the validity, interpretation, implementation or alleged material breach of any provision of this Agreement or regarding a question, including the questions as to whether the termination of this Agreement by one Party hereto has been legitimate, both Parties hereto shall endeavour to settle such dispute amicably. If the Parties fail to bring about an amicable settlement within a period of thirty (30) days, either Party to the dispute may give ten (10) days notice of invocation of dispute settlement by the Film Writers Association, Mumbai, to the other Party in writing.The Parties hereto shall submit to such mediation award by the Film Writers Association and the award shall be enforceable in any competent court of law in Mumbai.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree that any dispute arising under this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration conducted in Los Angeles, California, under the rules of the American Arbitration Association. Each party shall bear its own costs of arbitration.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 14 mandated mediation by the Film Writers Association in Mumbai. The changed version replaces this with binding arbitration in Los Angeles under AAA rules. This creates a direct contradiction about the dispute resolution process, creating uncertainty about which procedure to follow, thus creating an In-Text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If any dispute arises between the Parties hereto during the subsistence of this Agreement or thereafter, in connection with the validity, interpretation, implementation or alleged material breach of any provision of this Agreement or regarding a question, including the questions as to whether the termination of this Agreement by one Party hereto has been legitimate, both Parties hereto shall endeavour to settle such dispute amicably. If the Parties fail to bring about an amicable settlement within a period of thirty (30) days, either Party to the dispute may give ten (10) days notice of invocation of dispute settlement by the Film Writers Association, Mumbai, to the other Party in writing.The Parties hereto shall submit to such mediation award by the Film Writers Association and the award shall be enforceable in any competent court of law in Mumbai.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates mediation by the Film Writers Association in Mumbai. The changed text replaces this with binding arbitration in Los Angeles under AAA rules. This directly contradicts the original dispute resolution process, as parties are now obligated to use arbitration in LA instead of mediation in Mumbai. The contradicted text requires disputes to be settled amicably and then mediated by the Film Writers Association in Mumbai. The changed text makes that process impossible, creating a clear and direct conflict, thus creating an In-Text contradiction as it changes the dispute resolution process entirely.
